title: carex austroalpina
intro: Carex austroalpina, also known as Alpine Sedge, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Carex genus. It is native to the alpine regions of Europe and Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and prevent infection. Carex austroalpina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne.
